description:
Bring type-expr resolution in-house as a PURE READ. The resolver turns a type-expr AST into a hard Ty: structure (list, union, tuple, arrow) via compiler2 own Types; names via namespace lookup to TypeName to the already-present TypeDefined; scalars and builtins (integer, utf8, pid) minted directly with inner.

It never blocks, never demands, never mints a reference. The index-time reference walk (.1) already minted the names and the consumer (.4) already waited on their TypeDefined before calling in. A name not present here is a consumer-wiring bug, not a resolver retry -- that is the invariant that makes the per-call env-rebuild structurally unrepresentable.

No ModuleTypeEnv assembled -- the namespace is the env, prelude types visible by structural sharing. Grammar inspired by crate::type_expr::parse_type_expr but resolving via present facts, not a blob.

Verify: resolver unit tests over the worked examples (foo(SomeModule.t(float),integer); a plain alias; a refines brand), each handed a prebuilt TypeDefined set -- proving the resolver is a pure function of namespace plus present facts.
title: In-house type-expr resolver: name -> hard Ty via namespace + TypeDefined
